turns.user_message: I woke up this morning with this heavy, dull ache sitting right behind my ribs, and it just hasn't let up since. I tried to grab a coffee and read a book like I usually do, but my hands felt completely disconnected from whatever I was doing. It has only been a couple of days, but everything feels strangely muted and far away. I keep waiting for the feeling to pass so I can actually enjoy my morning again, but it just sits there. I guess my body is just tired, but it is weird how nothing seems to spark any real interest anymore.

That same heavy weight has been following me around all week now, and it is starting to wear me down. I used to look forward to my evening walks just to clear my head, but lately I can barely muster the energy to step outside. Even when I force myself to do things, it feels like I am just moving through thick water without any real purpose. The dullness in my chest has spread to everything I touch, turning simple routines into exhausting chores. I keep checking to see if the spark will come back on its own, but the quiet just keeps growing.

The numbness has completely taken over now, and I feel like I am carrying around a lead blanket that never comes off. I try to engage with the things around me, but my mind just slides right off everything without leaving a single trace. It is exhausting to pretend that I am fine when my whole body feels so hollow and disconnected from reality. Every single day blends into the next gray smear, and I am just watching it happen from behind a thick pane of glass. I keep hoping the physical weight will finally lift so I can feel something real again, but it just sinks deeper.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This just started recently.
